rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Cloud/remote features carry a Git dependency — out of scope.

rules_evaluated.evidence: line:96: '**Capability checks owed (before first delivery):** see `exercises/eval-loop.md` maintainer section: generation/judging agent separation, file-write reliability, judge immutability, and tactic rewrite reliability.'
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: LO at line 12 asserts 'generation and judging happen in separate agents' as a fact; maintainer block flags these capabilities as unverified-owed. The maintainer block is correct to flag; the student-facing LO should either be verified against the capability notes or qualified until the pre-delivery check runs.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Known correct Claude Code facts (subagents run in parallel, not concurrent with text output).
Sessions are the unit of work.

rules_evaluated.evidence: line:8: 'Ethan Mollick, "Garbage Can and Bitter Lesson."' (prework, student-facing, no URL, no source-type label). line:20: 'Did you read Ethan Mollick's *The Bitter Lesson versus The Garbage Can*?' (body, student-facing, no URL, no source-type label).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Mollick's One Useful Thing post is named as the source in the maintainer block (line 99) — add the URL to both the maintainer verification section and as a hyperlink on the prework/body citation. Source-type label is [practitioner direct].

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source-verification maintainer block on any student-facing file with practitioner citations.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:97-100: Source-verification section exists but is incomplete: (1) URL missing for Mollick — says 'add URL to the One Useful Thing post'; (2) no named fallback framing if Mollick source doesn't hold; (3) no freshness re-check instruction at delivery time.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Section exists — add the Mollick URL, a fallback framing ('if this post is no longer live, reframe as a practitioner question from any Mollick Newsletter issue'), and a freshness note ('re-verify URL at delivery; Mollick's newsletter is stable but check post is still public').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:18-19: 'Module 6 consumes `judges/groundedness-judge.md` from Module 5' — the module opens by consuming verification machinery (the judge file), but no smoke-test step is present in the module to confirm the judge fires before the loop starts.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The capability checks owed section (line 96) acknowledges judge immutability as unverified. A smoke-test prompt in the Start Here section — e.g. 'run the judge on a single synthetic round before the loop begins; confirm it returns a verdict and did not modify `judges/groundedness-judge.md`' — would satisfy rule 36 and serve as the pre-flight for the M5→M6 handoff. Alternatively, delegate the check to `exercises/eval-loop.md` Phase 0 and note the dependency.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path.
Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames (Agents 101-specific).

platform_claims_checked.claim: the main Claude session runs the loop, generation and judging happen in separate agents
Agents 101 runs on Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) and Cowork
generation/judging agent separation, file-write reliability, judge immutability, and tactic rewrite reliability (capability checks owed)

citations_checked.claim: Ethan Mollick, 'Garbage Can and Bitter Lesson' — prework reading
Ethan Mollick's *The Bitter Lesson versus The Garbage Can* — body reference
